title: Innovations in Aerospace Systems – Current Research (including Artificial Intelligence) and Future Challenges
description: Aircraft and spacecraft are highly complex systems, comprising various subsystems such as flight control, propulsion, and power. These subsystems feature Multiple Input & Multiple Output (MIMO) configurations and exhibit highly nonlinear characteristics.

title: Nonequilibrium reacting flows: First principles based modeling for chemical kinetics and hydrodynamics
description: Predicting state of the gas hitting vehicles flying at hypersonic speeds (Mach ~5) is challenging and is an exciting area of research. Hypersonic flows create shock waves, which compress and heat the surrounding gas to high-temperatures, nearly thousands of Kelvins.

title: Aerospace power as a critical tool of statecraft
description: Air Marshal TD Joseph examines aerospace power as a critical instrument of statecraft, highlighting its strategic, coercive, and diplomatic roles in modern conflict and international relations.
speaker: Air Marshal TD Joseph

title: Aerospace Engineering Open Day 2026
description: Indian Institute of Science, Bengaluru, as in the previous years, is organizing an OPEN DAY event to show-case its activities to the student community and the general public.

title: Statistical Discovery for Engineering and Science – a hands-on workshop using JMP®
description: We are happy announce a one-day hands-on workshop using JMP in the Auditorium of Department of Aerospace Engineering, IISc on 9th March.
